title: $1,000 in Bitcoin at Start of 2012
description: What $1,000 invested in Bitcoin on January 1, 2012 would be worth today. Bitcoin was trading at approximately $5.27. This was before any of the major bull runs, before mainstream awareness, and before most exchanges existed. The investment required holding through four separate 70-85% crashes.
custody_note: In 2012, self-custody via software wallets was the primary option. Most exchanges (Mt. Gox, Bitstamp) were primitive and many were hacked. This scenario assumes the coins were held securely throughout.
educational_note: The astronomical return is real in mathematical terms, but deeply misleading as a practical guide. Surviving four separate 77-85% crashes without selling, while also maintaining secure custody through a decade of exchange hacks and wallet risks, was extraordinarily difficult.
volatility_warning: Bitcoin has experienced multiple 80%+ peak-to-trough crashes. It is one of the most volatile assets in financial history.
